item.title: Wall Street Watchdogs Pause Some Cyber Exams After Mythos Shock
item.description: US regulators are pausing some cyber-related examinations of the largest banks, giving the lenders more breathing room as the firms dig into the risks exposed by Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model.

item.title: Now Even AI Is Seeing Growing AI Concentration Risk
item.description: It’s not just stocks.

item.title: AI Data Center Borrowing Rapidly Climbs Wall Street’s Risk List
item.description: Borrowing tied to the AI data-center boom is quickly climbing Wall Street’s list of potential credit threats, as investors increasingly worry that the breakneck pace of financing could sow the seeds of the next market shock.

item.title: Surging Bond Yields Add to Pressures Building for Fed’s Warsh
item.description: Kevin Warsh is poised to take the helm of the US central bank later this week facing pressure from the White House to deliver interest-rate cuts and growing resolve from his colleagues to stand pat. Now the bond market is piling on.

item.title: Canada’s Wealth Fund Is Not Aimed at Lowering Investors’ Taxes, Champagne Says
item.description: Canada’s finance minister said the country’s planned sovereign wealth fund will be designed to allow citizens to contribute to major nation-building projects, but isn’t intended to reduce their tax burdens.

item.title: Japan’s Finance Chief Pledges Bold Action for Weak Yen as Needed
item.description: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama indicated her resolve to intervene in a foreign exchange market to prop up the yen as needed as her Group of Seven counterparts understand the nation’s stance.

item.title: Musk Turns to Loyalists to Rebuild xAI Ahead of SpaceX IPO
item.description: Who's leading Musk's AI company? He's turned to leaders at his other companies to rebuild the startup.

item.title: Google Hires Staff From Bezos-Backed Contextual AI In Licensing Deal
item.description: Google DeepMind has reached a deal to hire more than 20 researchers from artificial intelligence startup Contextual AI and license its technology, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: JPMorgan’s CIO Sees Big Leadership Test as AI Fuels Major Shift
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. faces a daunting management challenge from artificial intelligence as the bank reaps productivity benefits while racing to contain a new set of risks, Global Chief Information Officer Lori Beer said.

item.title: Oil & Gas Dealmaking Heats Up With $20 Billion of Assets in Play
item.description: Buyout shops are finding a rare exit opportunity in the US oil patch.

item.title: SpaceX Going Public Is Igniting Wall Street’s Own Race to Orbit
item.description: As Elon Musk readies SpaceX for a highly anticipated initial public offering, Wall Street is racing to find the next big winner in space exploration.

item.title: SEC Floats Reducing Issuer Disclosures in Push for More IPOs
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission is seeking to boost initial public offerings with an overhaul to let new big issuers temporarily avoid many of the agency’s strongest disclosure rules.

item.title: UK to Allow Imports of Diesel, Jet Fuel Refined From Russian Oil
item.description: The UK will effectively loosen some Russian sanctions by allow imports of diesel and jet fuel refined from Moscow’s crude in countries like India and Turkey.

item.title: Google and Samsung Reveal Designs of Upcoming Smart Glasses
item.description: The companies are collaborating with eyewear makers Warby Parker and Gentle Monster

item.title: Google Revamps Search for AI Era, Debuts Coding Tools
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is redesigning its iconic search box and adding new artificial intelligence coding tools, the latest steps in the company’s multibillion-dollar campaign to expand influence in the age of AI.

item.title: Slow Data Center Hookups Offer US Power Grids Summer Relief
item.description: Key grids across the US have lowered their summer demand forecasts to reflect the slower-than-anticipated pace of data centers and other large connections, potentially helping avoid heat-related blackouts and energy shortages in the coming months.

item.title: Defense Group KNDS Plans to Cut its Stake in Gearbox Maker Renk
item.description: KNDS NV is looking to sell part of its stake in Renk Group AG as the Franco-German tankmaker prepares up for its own initial public offering.

item.title: Trump Team Seeks Longer Gas Tax Holiday With Phased-In Return
item.description: The Trump administration is working with Congress to suspend federal excise taxes on gasoline and diesel as the war in Iran drives up oil and fuel costs.

item.title: Chile Lowers Copper Production Outlook, Raises Price Forecasts
item.description: Top copper supplier Chile lowered its production forecasts for this year and next, reinforcing expectations of tight global supplies that have pushed prices near record highs.

item.title: US Open Stadium Seeks $400 Million Private Debt for Revamp Plans
item.description: The United States Tennis Association is in talks with institutional investors to borrow at least $400 million of private credit to refurbish New York’s Arthur Ashe Stadium.

item.title: Warsh Edges Closer to Divestment Promise Ahead of Swearing In
item.description: Kevin Warsh has sold most of the financial holdings he promised to divest from before becoming chair of the Federal Reserve, a key step in addressing a point of controversy surrounding his confirmation, a new government record showed.

item.title: Google to Unveil New AI and Search Features at Developers Conference
item.description: Members of the media view Google products during the Google I/O event in Mountain View, California. Photographer: Josh Edelson/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: Williams CEO Sees Gas Demand Outpacing Past Years on Powering AI
item.description: Demand for US natural gas is set to rise in the next 10 years more than it did for the past 15 years amid growing power needs from data centers supporting artificial intelligence and a global push for energy security, according to the head of Williams Cos.

item.title: Sony Launches $650 Headphones Made With Steel and Vegan Leather
item.description: Sony Group Corp. introduced a $650 pair of high-end wireless headphones, setting a new ceiling for the company in a segment that has already grown more pricey in recent years.

item.title: Euronext Results Beat on Volatility-Led Trading, Greek Exchange
item.description: Euronext NV’s results beat expectations as market volatility boosted trading volumes and the newly acquired Athens stock exchange contributed to revenue.

item.title: Apple’s New Hardware Chief Shakes Up Oversight of Product Design
item.description: Apple Inc. Chief Hardware Officer Johny Srouji is reorganizing hardware development and shifting oversight of key functions such as product design, part of an effort to speed up work on future devices.

item.title: BPCL Turns to Spot Crude as Iran Conflict Disrupts Term Supplies
item.description: India’s Bharat Petroleum Corp. Ltd. has largely shifted to making spot crude purchases as the Iran war upends annual arrangements for Middle Eastern barrels, highlighting challenges for refiners to secure supplies.

item.title: OpenAI Founding Member Andrej Karpathy Takes Role at Anthropic
item.description: Andrej Karpathy, a founding member of OpenAI who later went to work for Tesla Inc., has joined Anthropic PBC to do research and development focused on helping train new artificial intelligence models.

item.title: Fed’s Perli Says T-Bill Purchases Can Be Adjusted Up or Down
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York’s Roberto Perli said the central bank’s monthly Treasury bill purchases are not on a pre-determined course and can be adjusted depending on market conditions.

item.title: KKR and ECP Weigh Increasing Offer for Energy Group DCC
item.description: KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners are considering raising their offer to acquire energy provider DCC Plc, people with knowledge of the matter said.
